# MoveInAnimation

**Object**

The animation whereby a component moves in from the side of the screen.

## Declaration

```swift
object MoveInAnimation
```

## Discussion

With this animation type the component moves into view from the left or right side of the screen. Initially, the component is out of view as shown in [https://developer.apple.com/apple-news/videos/move-in.mp4].

You can configure a preferred starting position, but the position is not guaranteed. For example, if another component is blocking the preferred position, the `MoveInAnimation` attempts to start from the opposite side. If both sides are covered by other components, Apple News doesn’t apply the `MoveInAnimation`.

### Example

```json
{
  "components": [
    {
      "role": "heading1",
      "text": "2. Unbeatable Heat"
    },
    {
      "role": "figure",
      "URL": "bundle://figure.jpg",
      "animation": {
        "type": "move_in",
        "preferredStartingPosition": "left"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

## Related Documentation

- **Adding Animations**: Use animations to affect how parts of your article come into view the first time they appear.

## Animations

- **About Component Animations**: Learn how to affect the way in which components come into view.
- **ComponentAnimation**: Properties that all types of animations share.
- **AppearAnimation**: An animation type whereby a component appears on the screen.
- **FadeInAnimation**: The animation whereby a component fades into view.
- **ScaleFadeAnimation**: The animation in which a component scales up and fades into view.

## Inherits From

- ComponentAnimation
